creator: ggg_mail@inbox.ru
text:
Recently I experienced this issue of a fresh FreeBSD 8.1-RELEASE i386
 that boots from USB stick.

 Until I plugged off an USB CD-ROM drive the system booted all right,
 but after that moment I started to get "failed to mount root" messages.
 And, entering boot partition position (ufs:/dev/da0s1a) manually several
 seconds later, when it got detected, allo ws me to boot the system.
 
 I haven't tried to add 'kern.cam.boot_delay=10000' to loader.conf yet,
 but will try it as soon as I get to that FreeBSD box.
 
 Also, I think the bug http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=144824
 is the same as this one. There is also an explanation why my system
 booted OK until I unplugged the CDROM device.
